channel.title: Posts on Matthew Litwin's Personal Site
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Matthew Litwin's Personal Site

item.title: Preliminaries to a Software Testing Manifesto
item.description: I’m not quite ready for a manifesto. Still gathering my own thoughts, and trying to understand software testing terminology and methodologies. But - feels like there is a fair amount of confusion about how it’s supposed to work even in the best case. What is a Software Test A software test is a repeatable process applied to a software product that yields a binary pass/fail result. Testing Terminology Kinds/properties of tests Primary Taxonomy Unit Test, Component Test, Module Test - A test of a minimal part of a system that can be tested in isolation Solitary Unit Test - A test where the one and only concrete service is the service under test (all other dependencies, except for data/value types are mocked) Sociable Unit Test, Component Test - A test which uses concrete service dependencies to test the class currently under test.
